5a7166395542994082a3e814
What is the name of the fight song of the university whose main campus is in Lawrence, Kansas and whose branch campuses are in the Kansas City metropolitan area?
A: []
A: The answer is: The song "Rock Chalk, Jayhawk".
A: []
Q: What is the name of the fight song of the university whose main campus is in Lawrence, Kansas and whose branch campuses are in the Kansas City metropolitan area?
A: "The answer is: The song \"Rock Chalk, Jayhawk\". Rock Chalk, Jayhawk is the fight song and alma mater of the University of Kansas. The university's main campus is in Lawrence, Kansas and its branch campuses are in the Kansas City metropolitan area. (It should be noted that the university is often shortened to KU.)  The song was written in 1886 and is the university's official song."
A: The song \"Rock Chalk, Jayhawk\". Rock Chalk, Jayhawk is the fight song and alma mater of the University of Kansas. The university's main campus is in Lawrence, Kansas and its branch campuses are in the Kansas City metropolitan area. (It should be noted that the university is often shortened to KU.)  The song was written in 1886 and is the university's official song

5ae0361155429925eb1afc2c
Which  French ace pilot and adventurer fly L'Oiseau Blanc
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later. (in 2000)  
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later. (in 2000)   near the Atlantic coast of Ireland, the fuel tank recovered was identified as belonging to the Nungesser and Bich's L'Oiseau Blanc.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later. (in 2000)   near the Atlantic coast of Ireland, the fuel tank recovered was identified as belonging to the Nungesser and Bich's L'Oiseau Blanc. The theory is that the Nungesser, and Bich may have perished in the crash, and were not stranded, because the aircraft fuel tank is still there.  
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later. (in 2000)   near the Atlantic coast of Ireland, the fuel tank recovered was identified as belonging to the Nungesser and Bich's L'Oiseau Blanc. The theory is that the Nungesser, and Bich may have perished in the crash, and were not stranded, because the aircraft fuel tank is still there.   L'Oiseau Blanc is also known as Le Cicada or also called "La Guêpe".
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later. (in 2000)   near the Atlantic coast of Ireland, the fuel tank recovered was identified as belonging to the Nungesser and Bich's L'Oiseau Blanc. The theory is that the Nungesser, and Bich may have perished in the crash, and were not stranded, because the aircraft fuel tank is still there.   L'Oiseau Blanc is also known as Le Cicada or also called "La Guêpe". The aircraft that they flew, was a Breguet 19.  
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. They flew from Le Bourget, France, in an attempt to fly across the Atlantic Ocean. The flight did not succeed as they were not able to be seen by the French Navy, that had the large search ships, ocean rescue crews, and tracking squadrons and the aircraft disappeared near the coast of Ireland, along with L'Oiseau Blanc's fuel tank which turned up years later. (in 2000)   near the Atlantic coast of Ireland, the fuel tank recovered was identified as belonging to the Nungesser and Bich's L'Oiseau Blanc. The theory is that the Nungesser, and Bich may have perished in the crash, and were not stranded, because the aircraft fuel tank is still there.   L'Oiseau Blanc is also known as Le Cicada or also called "La Guêpe". The aircraft that they flew, was a Breguet 19.   
A: []
Q: Which  French ace pilot and adventurer fly L'Oiseau Blanc
A: "Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. L'Oiseau Blanc (French for The White Bird) was a modified F.B.19. Bich was a French engineer who later founded the Bic company. Nungesser, like Bich, was a Frenchman. He was born in 1892. Nungesser vanished in 1927. He was married twice and had two children with his first wife. N"
A: Marcel Bich and his friend and fellow aviator, Charles Nungesser flew L'Oiseau Blanc. L'Oiseau Blanc (French for The White Bird) was a modified F.B.19. Bich was a French engineer who later founded the Bic company. Nungesser, like Bich, was a Frenchman. He was born in 1892. Nungesser vanished in 1927. He was married twice and had two children with his first wife. N

A: In South West Pacific theatre of World War II, the Bristol Blenheim IF was used. The Bristol Blenheim is not jet-powered. The British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber used in the South West Pacific theatre of World War II is the Avro Lincoln. 
A: []
Q: Which British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber was used in the South West Pacific theatre of World War II?
A: "In South West Pacific theatre of World War II, the Bristol Blenheim IF was used. This was the last version of the British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber. So the answer is: Bristol Blenheim IF. (The Bristol Blenheim was a British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber. However, the \"B\" in \"Bl\" refers to  Bristol Aircraft. It is not a jet-powered. \"IF\" refers to one of the versions.)"
A: Bristol Blenheim IF. (The Bristol Blenheim was a British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber. However, the \"B\" in \"Bl\" refers to  Bristol Aircraft. It is not a jet-powered. \"IF\" refers to one of the versions.)

Q: Which Australian city founded in 1838 contains a boarding school opened by a Prime Minister of Australia and named after a school in London of the same name.
A: "The boarding school in the question is \"Geelong Grammar School\" in the city of Geelong in the state of Victoria in Australia. The school was opened in 1858 by Sir Henry Barkly, the Governor of Victoria at the time, in association with the Bishop of Melbourne. The name Geelong Grammar School is a derivative of \"Eton Grammar School\" which is a school in London, England. The school was established by Herbert Brodribb the headmaster of the school Eton"
A: The boarding school in the question is \"Geelong Grammar School\" in the city of Geelong in the state of Victoria in Australia. The school was opened in 1858 by Sir Henry Barkly, the Governor of Victoria at the time, in association with the Bishop of Melbourne. The name Geelong Grammar School is a derivative of \"Eton Grammar School\" which is a school in London, England. The school was established by Herbert Brodribb the headmaster of the school Eton

Q: D1NZ is a series based on what oversteering technique?
A: "D1NZ is a series of drifting. Drifting is an oversteer technique. The \"D1\" represents Drift 1 and \"NZ\" represents New Zealand. Drifting is a driving technique where the driver intentionally oversteers, creating a drift that the vehicle can maintain while following a set path. The driver of the car controls the drift angle using the steering wheel and throttle, typically by lifting off the throttle, then quickly reapplying it. This technique allows the car to"
A: D1NZ is a series of drifting. Drifting is an oversteer technique. The \"D1\" represents Drift 1 and \"NZ\" represents New Zealand. Drifting is a driving technique where the driver intentionally oversteers, creating a drift that the vehicle can maintain while following a set path. A: Orange. The name of the national holiday Koningsdag translates to King's Day. It is celebrated on April 27th, the birthday of the Dutch King. The name Oranjegekte translates to Orange Madness or Orange Craze. It is used to describe the massive outpouring of people wearing Orange clothing.

A: []
A: Orange. The name of the national holiday Koningsdag translates to King's Day. It is celebrated on April 27th, the birthday of the Dutch King. The name Oranjegekte translates to Orange Madness or Orange Craze. It is used to describe the massive outpouring of people wearing Orange clothing. So the answer is: Orange.

(Solution: Michael Woodworth Roberts, 1915–2007, was an American investigative journalist, director, and politician, member of the Arkansas House of Representatives from 1959 to 1967. He served as Chairman of the Republican Party of Arkansas from 1973 to 1975. After that period, Robert Lee Suettinger Jr. then had a 15-year career in the United States Intelligence Community. Prior to 2001, Mr. Suettinger was a Director of the Defense Intelligence Agency. In 2001 he was assigned as the U.S. National Intelligence Officer for East Asia. As of 2019, he is still an Active Intelligence Community member, albeit of a non-Governmental capacity, and has also served as a director of the global IT and Cybersecurity firm, BlackSky.

The relevant text was The 1925 Birthday Honours were appointments by King George V to various orders and honours to reward and honour good works by citizens of his Empire. The appointments, and the recommendations for them, were made to celebrate his official birthday, on 17 May 1925.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: There is no mention of this in the provided text. A related search turned up the 1925 Birthday Honours. The relevant text was The 1925 Birthday Honours were appointments by King George V to various orders and honours to reward and honour good works by citizens of his Empire. The appointments, and the recommendations for them, were made to celebrate his official birthday, on 17 May 1925. The King’s Birthday Honours had existed since 1860 but were for a period of ten years discontinued, starting in 1917 (except for the death honours and 1918 Jubilee honours), being revived by the government in 1925.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: There is no mention of this in the provided text. A related search turned up the 1925 Birthday Honours. The relevant text was The 1925 Birthday Honours were appointments by King George V to various orders and honours to reward and honour good works by citizens of his Empire. The appointments, and the recommendations for them, were made to celebrate his official birthday, on 17 May 1925. The King’s Birthday Honours had existed since 1860 but were for a period of ten years discontinued, starting in 1917 (except for the death honours and 1918 Jubilee honours), being revived by the government in 1925. So the answer is: 1864, as King George V was born in 1865 (on June 3) and his birthday is on June 3.

What type of activity does Owner earnings and Warren Buffett have in common?
A: []
A: Owner earnings and Warren Buffett both deal with accounting.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Owner earnings and Warren Buffett both deal with accounting. Owner earnings are a non-GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) metric for measuring the true economic earnings of a firm and are not typically presented in traditional accounting statements.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Owner earnings and Warren Buffett both deal with accounting. Owner earnings are a non-GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) metric for measuring the true economic earnings of a firm and are not typically presented in traditional accounting statements. Warren Buffett is a well-known investor, known for his value investing strategy, and is also known for his views on accounting and earnings.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Owner earnings and Warren Buffett both deal with accounting. Owner earnings are a non-GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) metric for measuring the true economic earnings of a firm and are not typically presented in traditional accounting statements. Warren Buffett is a well-known investor, known for his value investing strategy, and is also known for his views on accounting and earnings. So the answer is: accounting.

5a7dcc775542990b8f503ab5
Which component of shellite has the formula (ON)CHOH?
A: []
A: The formula (ON)CHOH can also be written as H2C(OH)NO.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The formula (ON)CHOH can also be written as H2C(OH)NO. In the Shawezewky — Snieprojek classification system, this formula is associated with the hydroxylamine.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The formula (ON)CHOH can also be written as H2C(OH)NO. In the Shawezewky — Snieprojek classification system, this formula is associated with the hydroxylamine. However in the case of shells, the component with the formula H2C(OH)NO is the said shellite compound also referred to as the CHOL (cyclic hydroxylamine).
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The formula (ON)CHOH can also be written as H2C(OH)NO. In the Shawezewky — Snieprojek classification system, this formula is associated with the hydroxylamine. However in the case of shells, the component with the formula H2C(OH)NO is the said shellite compound also referred to as the CHOL (cyclic hydroxylamine). Therefore, the answer to the question is: CHOL.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The formula (ON)CHOH can also be written as H2C(OH)NO. In the Shawezewky — Snieprojek classification system, this formula is associated with the hydroxylamine. However in the case of shells, the component with the formula H2C(OH)NO is the said shellite compound also referred to as the CHOL (cyclic hydroxylamine). Therefore, the answer to the question is: CHOL. 
A: []
Q: Which component of shellite has the formula (ON)CHOH?
A: "The formula (ON)CHOH can also be written as H2C(OH)NO. This is a component of urea. Shellite is another name for the urine mineral deposits that form on a whitening surface. So the answer is: urea. For explanation: (ON)CHOH = H2C(OH)NO = urea. Which is the component of shellite."
A: urea. For explanation: (ON)CHOH = H2C(OH)NO = urea. Which is the component of shellite

Q: Where is the Bubblegum Alley, that is similar to Gum Wall, located in California?
A: "There is no location in California like Gum Wall. In fact, Gum Wall is a wall in Seattle, Washington, covered with used gum. There is however, a location in Alameda, California, known as Bubblegum Alley, but it is not like the Gum Wall in Seattle."
A: There is no location in California like Gum Wall. In fact, Gum Wall is a wall in Seattle, Washington, covered with used gum. There is however, a location in Alameda, California, known as Bubblegum Alley, but it is not like the Gum Wall in Seattle

The movies Queen of Blood and Battle Beyond the Sun both contained scenes from what 1959 Soviet feature?
A: []
A: Battle Beyond the Sun is a 1960 science fiction film, made as a co-production between the Soviet Union and the United States.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Battle Beyond the Sun is a 1960 science fiction film, made as a co-production between the Soviet Union and the United States. It was edited down to create a film called Queen of Blood in the US.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Battle Beyond the Sun is a 1960 science fiction film, made as a co-production between the Soviet Union and the United States. It was edited down to create a film called Queen of Blood in the US. It was made from scenes of the 1959 Soviet film Planeta Bur, which was the original footage.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Battle Beyond the Sun is a 1960 science fiction film, made as a co-production between the Soviet Union and the United States. It was edited down to create a film called Queen of Blood in the US. It was made from scenes of the 1959 Soviet film Planeta Bur, which was the original footage. So the answer is: 1959 Soviet feature Planeta Bur.
A: []
Q: The movies Queen of Blood and Battle Beyond the Sun both contained scenes from what 1959 Soviet feature?
A: "Battle Beyond the Sun is a 1960 science fiction film, made as a co-production between the Soviet Union and the United States. It was made from a 1959 Soviet film, \"Battle Beyond the Sun\", which has not been released, but was re-released in 1998 and is available on DVD. The American version contains additional footage. So the answer is: the Soviet feature of \"Battle Beyond the Sun\"."
A: the Soviet feature of \"Battle Beyond the Sun\"

The kingdom of Dalriada was led by a line of kings, of which Aonghus Mór is the most famous, who conquered the western Highlands and Islands and ruled over a kingdom which covered a total land area of over 8300 km2. A: I do not know that.   But I can search that for you. It is actually MacConnolly. And the reference is from Wikipedia. Wikipedia Title: Argyll and Bute . The kingdom of Dalriada was led by a line of kings, of which Aonghus Mór is the most famous, who conquered the western Highlands and Islands and ruled over a kingdom which covered a total land area of over 8300 km2. The dynasty survived until it was overthrown by Kenneth MacAlpin, the founder of the Kingdom of Scotland. It was centred at Dál Riata in the north of Ireland and Argyll and Bute in the west of Scotland. Kenneth MacAlpin incorporated Argyll and Bute into his Kingdom of Scotland and the dynasty of Dál Riata was eventually overthrown.

The song "Chim Chim Cher-ee" was in "Mary Poppins" which was released in 1964. The "It's a Small World" ride opened in 1966. The answer to this question, is 1966. But not the exact answer is 1966 because of this little information that the "It's a Small World" ride, opened in 1966 after its sister ride "Mary Poppins" the song featured in which opened in 1964, was opened two years after the release of the movie.

A: When Hingle got older, he was diagnosed with Graves' disease, and had to sell his ranch in Montana, USA. His close friend, William Conrad, got him a job working with his friend, from whom Hingle "learned everything he knew." It was this friend who went on to achieve great success in the popular TV series: "Maverick".
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: When Hingle got older, he was diagnosed with Graves' disease, and had to sell his ranch in Montana, USA. His close friend, William Conrad, got him a job working with his friend, from whom Hingle "learned everything he knew." It was this friend who went on to achieve great success in the popular TV series: "Maverick". So the answer is: "Maverick".

Q: Martin Patterson "Pat" Hingle was a close friend of an actor who achieved success in what Western TV series?
A: "Pat Hingle and the late actor \"Gil\" Gerard. The Gil Gerard starred in the TV series Buck Rogers. The other option that fit would be the TV series Hardcastle and McCormick, and the more famous TV series that fits better is: Magnum P.I.'s, Pfc., Earl Simon but better is yet another, of which we are not even certain, however one other we do know for a fact is the actor: Private Kelly of Kelly's Heroes, Marvin Hagler's buddy"
A: Pat Hingle and the late actor \"Gil\" Gerard. The Gil Gerard starred in the TV series Buck Rogers. The other option that fit would be the TV series Hardcastle and McCormick, and the more famous TV series that fits better is: Magnum P.I.'s, Pfc., Earl Simon but better is yet another, of which we are not even certain, however one other we do know for a fact is the actor: Private Kelly of Kelly's Heroes, Marvin Hagler's buddy

Q: What material did a hairdresser from Yorkshire, England invent that was named by his granddaughter?
A: "A hairdresser from Yorkshire, England, invented Hertford Feathers in the 1970s, but there is no evidence of the Hertford Feathers being named by a hairdresser. However, a hairdresser named Charles Rennie Macintosh, also invented something called the Hertford Feathers; this is another misconception; Macintosh invented the 'hair dryers' but more famously, the under-cup hair dryer was actually invented by a Clever man, Scott"
A: A hairdresser from Yorkshire, England, invented Hertford Feathers in the 1970s, but there is no evidence of the Hertford Feathers being named by a hairdresser. However, a hairdresser named Charles Rennie Macintosh, also invented something called the Hertford Feathers; this is another misconception; Macintosh invented the 'hair dryers' but more famously, the under-cup hair dryer was actually invented by a Clever man, Scott

Q: What arcade game is named after the only player in major league history to have a 0.300 batting average for 7 consecutive seasons?
A: "Cap Anson is the only player in major league history to have a 0.300 batting average for 7 consecutive seasons. Anson was a former professional baseball player. Anson played for the Rockford Forest Citys of the Northwestern League in 1871. He also played for the Chicago White Stockings from 1876 to 1897. In the 1890s Anson played for the Chicago Colts. Anson played in the Chicago Colt's inaugural game in 1890"
A: Cap Anson is the only player in major league history to have a 0.300 batting average for 7 consecutive seasons. Anson was a former professional baseball player. Anson played for the Rockford Forest Citys of the Northwestern League in 1871. He also played for the Chicago White Stockings from 1876 to 1897. In the 1890s Anson played for the Chicago Colts. Anson played in the Chicago Colt's inaugural game in 1890

A: No information found about this place Salisbury Woodland Gardens. However an answer was found for The Woodland Gardens. The Woodland Gardens, located in Bearwood, Kidderminster, Shropshire, England, is a former zoo site which links a zoo with a park.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: No information found about this place Salisbury Woodland Gardens. However an answer was found for The Woodland Gardens. The Woodland Gardens, located in Bearwood, Kidderminster, Shropshire, England, is a former zoo site which links a zoo with a park. The Woodland Gardens were designed and built under the watchful eye of Dr Gerald Hill, who later became Vice President of the International Association of Zoological Gardens and Aquariums (IAZA), in 1979.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: No information found about this place Salisbury Woodland Gardens. However an answer was found for The Woodland Gardens. The Woodland Gardens, located in Bearwood, Kidderminster, Shropshire, England, is a former zoo site which links a zoo with a park. The Woodland Gardens were designed and built under the watchful eye of Dr Gerald Hill, who later became Vice President of the International Association of Zoological Gardens and Aquariums (IAZA), in 1979. So the answer is: Dr Gerald Hill.

A: The name "Mountbatten-Windsor" was introduced in 1960 when Queen Elizabeth II gave up her titles as a German princess of the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha after a vote of the British House of Commons which then adopted a new dynastic name of House of Windsor which is part of the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha's rival branch of the European House of Wettin's royal branch the House of Battenberg, renamed "House of Battenberg"
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The name "Mountbatten-Windsor" was introduced in 1960 when Queen Elizabeth II gave up her titles as a German princess of the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha after a vote of the British House of Commons which then adopted a new dynastic name of House of Windsor which is part of the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha's rival branch of the European House of Wettin's royal branch the House of Battenberg, renamed "House of Battenberg" to House of Mountbatten in 1917 when Prince Louis of Battenberg married the soon-to-be-queen.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The name "Mountbatten-Windsor" was introduced in 1960 when Queen Elizabeth II gave up her titles as a German princess of the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha after a vote of the British House of Commons which then adopted a new dynastic name of House of Windsor which is part of the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha's rival branch of the European House of Wettin's royal branch the House of Battenberg, renamed "House of Battenberg" to House of Mountbatten in 1917 when Prince Louis of Battenberg married the soon-to-be-queen. Therefore, the answer is: The House of Battenberg (or its 1917 renamed name of the House of Mountbatten) is part of the House of Wettin.
A: []
Q: The name "Mountbatten-Windsor" applies to male-line descendants of the Queen without royal styles and titles which is a Eurpean dynasty resulting in what family?
A: "The name \"Mountbatten-Windsor\" is used by the descendants of the Queen without a royal style or title who are the members of the British Royal Family. The family is the House of Windsor. The House of Windsor is a member of the European Royal Family, the House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha was a European royal house in the United Kingdom, and the European royal house is a type of the European Royal Family. So the answer is: European Royal Family.."
A: European Royal Family.

Q: What was the name of the fanzine that interviewed the Sex Pistols bassist when he was in the band "The Flowers of Romance?"
A: "The fanzine is not specified. However, Rudi Kloser has written a fanzine called \"The Flowers of Romance\", which he began publishing in 1979 in San Francisco, and in the first issue he interviewed Glen Matlock of the Sex Pistols in a recent band, the \"Glad Aliens\".  So the answer is: \"The Flowers of Romance.\""
A: "The Flowers of Romance."

5adccdd55542992c1e3a2408
Meaning "reddish water" in Hawaiian, which location was Butch Van Artsdalen known for surfing 25-foot waves at?
A: []
A: In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition. These huge waves led to the great innovation in wetsuits which began in 1961 in Laguna Beach with Tom Blake.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition. These huge waves led to the great innovation in wetsuits which began in 1961 in Laguna Beach with Tom Blake. Greg Noll was first to surf waves over 20 ft and Butch Van Artsdalen was first to surf waves over 25 ft
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition. These huge waves led to the great innovation in wetsuits which began in 1961 in Laguna Beach with Tom Blake. Greg Noll was first to surf waves over 20 ft and Butch Van Artsdalen was first to surf waves over 25 ft (likely the most) over any known swell in the world.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition. These huge waves led to the great innovation in wetsuits which began in 1961 in Laguna Beach with Tom Blake. Greg Noll was first to surf waves over 20 ft and Butch Van Artsdalen was first to surf waves over 25 ft (likely the most) over any known swell in the world. The Cape is now home to the gnarly waves that it's known for, earning it the nickname, 'Big Red' - because of it's reddish (reddish-pink) colored water, however Butch is more famously known for surfing Makena Beach, also known as Big Red.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition. These huge waves led to the great innovation in wetsuits which began in 1961 in Laguna Beach with Tom Blake. Greg Noll was first to surf waves over 20 ft and Butch Van Artsdalen was first to surf waves over 25 ft (likely the most) over any known swell in the world. The Cape is now home to the gnarly waves that it's known for, earning it the nickname, 'Big Red' - because of it's reddish (reddish-pink) colored water, however Butch is more famously known for surfing Makena Beach, also known as Big Red. So the answer is: Makena Beach, also known as Big Red. Which is commonly nicknamed 'Big Red' meaning "reddish water" in Hawaiian.
A: []
Q: Meaning "reddish water" in Hawaiian, which location was Butch Van Artsdalen known for surfing 25-foot waves at?
A: "In 1960, Greg Noll and Butch Van Artsdalen surfed the waves of Cape San Lucas at 25 feet (for the set wave) while on a freediving expedition. So the answer is: Cape San Lucas."
A: Cape San Lucas

A: Not available in the given sources. The sources do not mention Eugene Habecker as the chairman of any magazine. These sources mention Eugene Habecker as the chairman of the Christian Reformed Church. He has been the chairman of the Christian Reformed Church in North America (CRCNA) since 2004. Since 2013, he is serving as the president of the CRCNA and not as chairman of the denomination. In the fall of 2008 he served as interim executive director for the Calvin College Grand Rapids, Michigan chapter of the World Vision Global 6K for Water Fundraiser. In 2009 he was interim president at Dordt College in Sioux Center, Iowa. In 2015-2016, he was interim vice president of academic affairs at Cornerstone University in Grand Rapids, Michigan.

Since 2013, he is serving as the president of the CRCNA and not as chairman of the denomination. In the fall of 2008 he served as interim executive director for the Calvin College Grand Rapids, Michigan chapter of the World Vision Global 6K for Water Fundraiser. In 2009 he was interim president at Dordt College in Sioux Center, Iowa. In 2015-2016, he was interim vice president of academic affairs at Cornerstone University in Grand Rapids, Michigan. Eugene Habecker is currently professor of New Testament at Cornerstone University.

A: The Treaty of Gandamak. Initially under the leadership of Ayub Khan but later under Abdur Rahman Khan the Emirate of Afghanistan resisted the British presence in the region and the threat of further military incursions. The Treaty of Gandamak officially ended the First Afghan War, ending the British occupation of Afghanistan from 1880. The treaty recognized Afghan independence and removed the previous requirement of Afghan acceptance of British supremacy, in exchange for territory and key treaty obligations (refer p. 334 for specific details).

A: []

A: The first option, "hot toddy" is typically associated with an alcoholic mixed drink containing rum or brandy that is usually consumed before going to bed, primarily when the drinker is ill. A: The first option, "hot toddy" is typically associated with an alcoholic mixed drink containing rum or brandy that is usually consumed before going to bed, primarily when the drinker is ill.   The second option, "Kir" or "Kir Royale" is a classic cocktail made with cassis (blackcurrant liqueur) and champagne, that is traditionally drunk at parties and celebrations. So the answer is: Hot toddy.

However, a \"Kir Royale\" is made with champagne instead of white wine. So, no. (The name is derived"

5adf430e5542993a75d2645e
Which canal, Miami Canal or Dundee Canal, also supplies hydro-power and water for manufacturing?
A: []
A: The answer is the Dundee Canal.
A: []
Q: Which canal, Miami Canal or Dundee Canal, also supplies hydro-power and water for manufacturing?
A: "The answer is the Dundee Canal. The Dundee Canal was constructed by the Canadian engineering company, Mabey & Johnson, and supplies hydro-power and water for manufacturing as well as transportation. (Note: Mabey & Johnson, though most famous for their Mabey Bridge-building business, was founded in Dundee and the canal is almost certain to be in the Ottawa River Valley near Ottawa, Canada)\u2026"
A: the Dundee Canal. The Dundee Canal was constructed by the Canadian engineering company, Mabey & Johnson, and supplies hydro-power and water for manufacturing as well as transportation. (Note: Mabey & Johnson, though most famous for their Mabey Bridge-building business, was founded in Dundee and the canal is almost certain to be in the Ottawa River Valley near Ottawa, Canada)\u2026
